etymology.description: 𡱂 (phonetic, *duːn, buttocks) + 殳 (semantic, hand holding a weapon) -> sound of hitting -> later used for hall
etymology.interpretation: The sound element 𡱂 originally depicted 'buttocks' and phonetic value, combined with 殳 conveying the sense of using a tool. Though the original meaning related to hitting, 殿 (diàn, 'hall') was later extended to large ceremonial halls, likely through a borrowing process where the sound was prioritized over any concrete semantic relationship with initial 'hitting' imagery.

examples.chinese: 故宫里的殿(故宮裡的殿)非常壮观(非常壯觀)。
examples.pinyin: Gùgōng lǐ de diàn fēicháng zhuàngguān.
examples.english: The hall in the Forbidden City is very magnificent.

examples.chinese: 在长安(在長安)，宫殿的建筑风格独特(宮殿的建築風格獨特)。
examples.pinyin: Zài Cháng'ān, gōngdiàn de jiànzhù fēnggé dútè.
examples.english: In Chang'an, the architectural style of the palace hall is unique.

examples.chinese: 许多人来到这座大殿(許多人來到這座大殿)祈祷(祈禱)。
examples.pinyin: Xǔduō rén lái dào zhè zuò dàdiàn qídǎo.
examples.english: Many people come to this grand hall to pray.
